content.full_text:
QUACKENBUSH 
January 6, 1986 
FOR 22nd ASSEMBL Y 
,,1· D~ I 
FPPC Advice No. A-&6-~ 
Confirmed by telephone. 
'81 
7 
Carla Wardlow, Political Reform 
Consultant 
Carla Wardlow 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J. Street 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Carla: 
This letter is to confirm our recent conversations on the 
matter of two $200.00 receipts by the committee that are 
a bit out of the ordinary, and your advised reporting 
procedures for each. 
As you recall, both $200.00 receipts were received from 
our 1986 primary opponent's staff people who were arrested 
for stealing Quackenbush campaign signs. 
As a result of 
a civil compromise 
agreed upon by the staff people and 
Chuck Quackenbush, each staff person was to pay $200.00 
to the committee (the committee was the victimized party) 
for restitution for the crime. 
Through our conversations, you advised me on the following: 
1. 
Both $200.00 receipts are to be reported as 
"Miscellaneous Adjustments to cash position". 
An explanation of the receipts will be included 
on the FPPC report. 
2. 
The one receipt which was cash was deemed legal and 
receivable by you in that it was not a "contri-
bution" . 
If there are any further questions regarding this matter, 
please call me. 
Our new committee phone number is (408/ 
866-5353) . 
erely, 
£ 
lCf 
J'~nice Ploeger 
Qmpaign Manager 
12280 Saratoga-Sunnyvale Road • Saratoga, CA 95070 • (408) 446-5676 
Pard for hy Quackcnbu,>h for A:.\cmbly Commrttcc. Alvin Royse, Trca.'.Urcr

sections.question_synthetic: How should two $200 restitution payments received from opposing campaign staff members be reported on FPPC forms, and are these payments considered legal contributions?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming letter from a campaign (Quackenbush for Assembly) to FPPC staff confirming prior telephone advice, not an FPPC advice letter response. The document references prior conversations with FPPC consultant but does not contain formal Q/A sections. Dated January 6, 1986 but references 1987 document ID; appears to be a confirmatory/follow-up letter rather than a formal advice request or response.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: How should two $200 restitution payments received from opposing campaign staff members be reported on FPPC forms, and are these payments considered legal contributions?

Street Sacramento, CA 95814 Dear Carla: This letter is to confirm our recent conversations on the matter of two $200.00 receipts by the committee that are a bit out of the ordinary, and your advised reporting procedures for each. As you recall, both $200.00 receipts were received from our 1986 primary opponent's staff people who were arrested for stealing Quackenbush campaign signs. As a result of a civil compromise agreed upon by the staff people and Chuck Quackenbush, each staff person was to pay $200.00 to the committee (the committee was the victimized party) for restitution for the crime. Through our conversations, you advised me on the following: 1. Both $200.00 receipts are to be reported as "Miscellaneous Adjustments to cash position". An explanation of the receipts will be included on the FPPC report. 2. The one receipt which was cash was deemed legal and receivable by you in that it was not a "contri- bution" . If there are any further questions regarding this matter, please call me. embedding.summary: A campaign manager writes to FPPC consultant Carla Wardlow to confirm telephone advice regarding the reporting of two $200 restitution payments received from opposing campaign staff who were arrested for stealing campaign signs, confirming these should be reported as 'Miscellaneous Adjustments to cash position' with explanatory notes.
